posts.headline: Account argues Mansour Barzani DOJ case is durable image damage for the KDP regardless of legal outcome
posts.summary: KurdistanWatch posts an analytical thread saying the new US bribery case against Mansour Barzani functions as a reputational anchor on the KDP brand whether or not the civil forfeiture ultimately succeeds in court, since the underlying DLA fuel-scheme narrative is now in the public record.

posts.headline: More detail surfaces on the Mansour Barzani Beverly Hills mansion purchase trail in the DOJ filing
posts.summary: Thread highlights additional specifics from the Justice Department filing on the Beverly Hills mansion buy, including renovation and shell-company structuring identified in the complaint, and the role of the Virginia-based contractor referenced as Contractor 1 in the underlying jet-fuel scheme.

posts.headline: KurdistanWatch points to additional DOJ documentation on the Barzani fuel-contract case
posts.summary: Follow-on post directing readers to expanded Justice Department materials on the Operation Inherent Resolve fuel-supply scheme, the 25 cent per litre alleged bribe channel, and the procurement governance angle now in front of US prosecutors.

posts.headline: Monte Carlo Doualiya: Washington informs Baghdad the Trump administration intends to designate Hashd al-Shaabi as a terrorist organisation
posts.summary: Account cites Monte Carlo Doualiya International radio reporting that Washington has formally notified Baghdad of an intent to put a blanket Foreign Terrorist Organisation designation on the Popular Mobilization Forces, a sharp escalation beyond the existing brigade-by-brigade approach. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Coordination Framework Friday session at Hammoudi's home ends without naming a PM candidate
posts.summary: Account confirms in real time that the Friday meeting at Sheikh Hammam Hammoudi's residence broke up without producing a PM nominee, leaving Iraq inside the final 24 hour window before the constitutional deadline. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Photos of Coordination Framework leaders arriving at Hammoudi's residence to settle the PM file
posts.summary: Visual report of CF principals converging on Hammoudi's house Friday evening for the decisive PM session that subsequently broke up without an agreement. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: CNN per Pakistani and Iranian sources: Iran is sending a delegation to Islamabad
posts.summary: Account flags CNN reporting via Pakistani and Iranian sources that Iran is dispatching a delegation to Islamabad, framed as part of the post-ceasefire diplomatic track now running in parallel to the secondary-sanctions push. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Kuwait announces it was struck by two drones launched from Iraqi territory
posts.summary: Account picks up the Kuwaiti Defence Ministry statement on two explosive-laden drones from Iraq hitting Kuwait's northern border posts, framed as the third bilateral incident in two months. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Israeli media: Netanyahu underwent surgery to remove a cancerous tumour
posts.summary: Account relays Israeli press reporting that PM Benjamin Netanyahu underwent a procedure to remove a cancerous tumour, a regional governance variable worth flagging for the next round of Israeli decisions on the Syria and Lebanon files. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Quote tweet boosting a recorded analysis of the current Iraqi political and economic moment
posts.summary: Quote tweet directing followers to a long-form audio report that the account frames as the cleanest available read on Iraq's combined political and economic squeeze in the days before the PM deadline. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Account warns Iraq is now inside the targeting circle as the regional war expands
posts.summary: Original post arguing that the Kuwait drone incident, paired with the broader regional posture, places Iraq squarely inside the targeting calculus of the next phase of the war. Opinion call rather than confirmed reporting. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: Knights flags the Iraqi oil sector to Treasury Secretary Bessent in the secondary-sanctions context
posts.summary: Quote tweet directly addressing Secretary Bessent and the US Treasury, urging Washington not to overlook Iraq's oil sector inside the new China-and-shippers Iran sanctions package. Frames Iraqi crude logistics as the next obvious enforcement gap.

posts.headline: Knights notes Houthis released scores of detainees, asks what the next operational priority looks like
posts.summary: Quote tweet on the Houthis releasing scores of detainees, with Knights flagging the move as a tell on the post-ceasefire bandwidth Sanaa has now opened up for other regional fronts.

posts.headline: Knights pushes back on the framing that Sudani is the worst recent Iraqi PM
posts.summary: Quote-tweet thread arguing that against the universe of post-2003 Iraqi premiers, Sudani's record on militia management and macro stewardship reads better than the current discourse implies, a useful read inside the live PM selection debate.

posts.headline: Knights wonders aloud whether the next US enforcement focus shifts to SAF in Sudan
posts.summary: Quote tweet speculating that after the Iran sanctions wave, Washington's next operational focus could pivot to the Sudanese Armed Forces, a sequencing call that matters for any Iraqi or Gulf-linked logistics chains touching Khartoum.
